meaning: n.优秀人材,杰出者adj.优秀人材的,杰出者的
examples: He found the school very cliquey and elitist.
He was an elitist who had no time for the masses.
He worries about a time when college athletics become even more elitist than they are now.
By both word and deed, Obama has firmly established himself as an unapologetic elitist.
"We're a little more democratic than Sony or Philips, which are elitist, " says Kim.
